content: We can reframe biological/chemical agents (from Topic B's definition) as naturally occurring soft Q-learning agents that evolve to maximize the entropy of their functional effects, rather than just optimizing for a single fixed fitness or reaction outcome. This means biological agents like viruses or bacteria do not just evolve to maximize pure replication or metabolic efficiency, but also retain enough functional diversity to adapt to changing host or environmental conditions via the same maximum entropy objective that underpins soft Q-learning.
